qa.overall_assessment: Iteration 4 resolves both blocking issues from iter3: AC9 false-positive grep and AC10 multiline-args grep. However, Codex adversarial review surfaces one new blocking spec_text_vs_execution_drift gap in AC10: the THEN clause asserts tilde form 'NOT /root/.claude/' but the check object provides no recipe to verify that the /root/ form is ABSENT after dev applies M13. A dev could add the tilde form alongside the existing /root/ form and all three AC10 checks would pass while M13 is violated. One minimal AC check addition is required before dev proceeds.

issues.title: AC10 THEN asserts /root/ form absent but check object provides no negative recipe to verify that
issues.description: AC10 THEN clause reads: 'grep -F "python3 ~/.claude/scripts/execute-push.py" commands/push.md returns at least one match (tilde form, NOT /root/.claude/)'. The 'NOT /root/.claude/' qualifier is part of the literal assertion. However, the check object supplies only: (1) invocation_uses_tilde_form — a positive grep for the tilde form; (2) invocation_contains_all_args — grep -A8 for continuation args; (3) old_and_pattern_absent_exit_code — checks for '&& push.sh' / 'validate_sentinel.py' / 'bash push.sh' patterns. None of these three checks verifies that grep -F 'python3 /root/.claude/scripts/execute-push.py' commands/push.md exits 1. The current pre-dev commands/push.md line 183 already contains exactly 'python3 /root/.claude/scripts/execute-push.py ...' (confirmed live: exit 0). If dev adds the tilde-form invocation block alongside the existing /root/ form, all three AC10 checks pass — invocation_uses_tilde_form is satisfied, grep -A8 finds the continuation args in the new block, old_and_pattern_absent passes — yet the /root/ form remains in the file and M13 is violated. The THEN text's 'NOT /root/.claude/' assertion is mechanically unverifiable by any of the three supplied check recipes.
issues.evidence: Live greps against /dev/shm/dev-workspace/dot-claude/commands/push.md: (1) grep -F '/root/.claude/scripts/execute-push.py' commands/push.md → exit 0, match at line 183: 'python3 /root/.claude/scripts/execute-push.py --request-id "$REQUEST_ID" --repo-hash "$REPO_HASH" --remote "$RESOLVED_REMOTE"'. (2) grep -A8 -F 'python3 ~/.claude/scripts/execute-push.py' commands/push.md → exit 1 (tilde form not yet present). This confirms the /root/ form exists and tilde form does not; dev will add the tilde form but is not forced by any AC check to remove the /root/ form.
issues.proposed_fix:
Add a fourth entry to the AC10 check object: '"old_root_form_absent_exit_code": 1' with verification command 'grep -F "python3 /root/.claude/scripts/execute-push.py" commands/push.md' (must exit 1 — no match). This directly verifies the THEN text's 'NOT /root/.claude/' qualifier. The corrected check object should contain all four assertions:
  1. invocation_uses_tilde_form (positive grep for tilde form — existing)
  2. invocation_contains_all_args / invocation_args_verification_command grep -A8 (existing)
  3. old_and_pattern_absent_exit_code: 1 (existing)
  4. old_root_form_absent_exit_code: 1, verification_command: "grep -F 'python3 /root/.claude/scripts/execute-push.py' commands/push.md" (NEW)

iter3_issues_resolved.original_issue: ISSUE-1 (iter3): AC9 negative grep 'grep -E os\.unlink|rm -f.*grant|unlink.*grant' matched prose 'MUST NOT ... unlink the push-analyst grant manually' at commands/push.md line 146, producing exit 0 (false positive) when exit 1 was required.
iter3_issues_resolved.resolution: Iter4 AC9 check replaced with awk code-fence scoped check: 'awk '/```/{in_code=!in_code} in_code{print}' commands/push.md | grep -E 'os\.unlink\(|rm -f .*grant''. The tighter pattern requires literal paren after unlink (os.unlink() form), and prose 'unlink the push-analyst grant' does not contain a paren. The fence filter additionally excludes all prose lines.
iter3_issues_resolved.verification: Live command: awk '/```/{in_code=!in_code} in_code{print}' /dev/shm/dev-workspace/dot-claude/commands/push.md | grep -E 'os\.unlink\(|rm -f .*grant' → exit 1 (no match). Confirmed resolved.

iter3_issues_resolved.original_issue: ISSUE-2 (iter3): AC10 plain 'grep execute-push.py' returned only the script-name line (ending with backslash continuation), which does not contain --branch, --request-id, --repo-hash, or --remote on that single line. Assertion that grep output contains all four arg names was mechanically impossible for multiline format.
iter3_issues_resolved.resolution: Iter4 AC10 replaced plain grep with 'grep -A8 -F 'python3 ~/.claude/scripts/execute-push.py' commands/push.md', capturing 8 lines after the match line. The 4-arg invocation block (4 continuation lines) and 5-arg --auto variant (5 continuation lines) both fit within 8 lines. The check object now specifies invocation_args_verification_command with the -A8 form and invocation_args_must_contain: ['--branch', '--request-id', '--repo-hash', '--remote'].
iter3_issues_resolved.verification: Simulation: a 4-line multiline invocation block with 'python3 ~/.claude/scripts/execute-push.py \' on line 1 and '--repo-hash ...', '--branch ...', '--remote ...', '--request-id ...' on lines 2-5 — grep -A8 captures all 5 lines. Confirmed resolved.

passed_checks.title: Script interface completeness: required args and no --pre-head
passed_checks.details: M2 lists all required args: --repo-hash, --branch, --remote, --request-id, [--auto]. M3 states SESSION_ID is read from CLAUDE_SESSION_ID env var. No --pre-head arg appears anywhere in ticket, context JSON, or AC JSON. Single HEAD drift check at step 9 (grant.head_sha vs git rev-parse HEAD) is the only HEAD comparison.

passed_checks.title: 13-step operation order is internally consistent
passed_checks.details: Parse args → read SESSION_ID → compute grant path → check push.sh → read grant → parse JSON → validate non-head_sha fields → act on verdict → check HEAD drift → consume grant → write sentinel → set env → os.execv. Each step's abort condition is consistent with its position. Steps 1-4 are infrastructure checks (exit 2). Steps 5-9 are validation (exit 1, grant NOT consumed). Steps 10-13 are commit+exec sequence (grant consumed at step 10, sentinel written at step 11, execv at step 13). No contradictions found. Unchanged from iter3.

passed_checks.title: Exit code taxonomy consistent across M3/M4/M11 and all ACs
passed_checks.details: Exit 2 = infrastructure failures: SESSION_ID unset (M3/AC8/step 2), push.sh absent/not-executable (M4/AC6/step 4), execv OSError (M11/AC7/step 13). Exit 1 = validation failures: grant absent (step 5), parse fail (step 6), field mismatch (M5/M6/AC2/steps 7-8), blocked verdict (M8/AC4/step 8), HEAD drift (M7/AC3/step 9), consume failure (step 10). No contradictions found. Unchanged from iter3.

passed_checks.title: Grant preserved on all steps 5-9 failures
passed_checks.details: Steps 5-9 each explicitly state 'grant NOT consumed'. Grant consume is deferred to step 10, which is only reached after steps 5-9 all pass. ACs 2, 3, 4 all check grant_still_exists=true. Step 5 failure (grant file not found) never touches the file. Unchanged from iter3.

passed_checks.title: Test isolation: HOME=<tmpdir> and MOCK_CAPTURE_PATH
passed_checks.details: test_isolation_contract in AC JSON: 'All ACs that require a mock push.sh use HOME=<tmpdir> isolation. Mock push.sh at <tmpdir>/.claude/hooks/push.sh. Script invoked by absolute path /dev/shm/dev-workspace/dot-claude/scripts/execute-push.py. NEVER write to ~/.claude/hooks/push.sh. Mock uses MOCK_CAPTURE_PATH env var (not EXECUTE_PUSH_TEST_CAPTURE). Mock must NOT write to stderr.' All ACs with mock push.sh use HOME=<tmpdir> in their GIVEN and WHEN clauses. Unchanged from iter3.

passed_checks.title: Sentinel schema: exactly 5 keys matching push.sh consumer
passed_checks.details: AC1 check specifies sentinel_exact_keys: ['result', 'request_id', 'head', 'branch', 'remote'] and sentinel_result='PASS'. This matches push.sh lines 137-150 which verify exactly these 5 fields. Branch encoding: branch.replace('/', '__') for path, raw branch in JSON 'branch' field. Unchanged from iter3.

passed_checks.title: AC6 grant preservation on push.sh absent consistent with step 4
passed_checks.details: Step 4 (push.sh check) precedes step 5 (grant file read). A step-4 failure exits before any grant file is touched. AC6 checks grant_still_exists=true and sentinel_absent=true. All three sources consistent. Unchanged from iter3.

passed_checks.title: AC7 execv OSError mechanism is reliable on Linux
passed_checks.details: Non-existent shebang interpreter (#!/tmp/execute-push-nointerp-<pid>) causes os.execv to raise OSError/FileNotFoundError (ENOENT). M11 sentinel cleanup path triggers: sentinel is unlinked before exit 2. Grant was consumed at step 10 before sentinel write, so grant IS absent. Confirmed by iter3 Codex live probe (returncode=2, FileNotFoundError). Unchanged from iter3.

passed_checks.title: AC5 subprocess.run stderr capture valid across os.execv
passed_checks.details: fd 2 (stderr) survives os.execv as an open file descriptor. subprocess.run(stderr=subprocess.PIPE) pipe connected to subprocess fd 2 is inherited by execv'd process image. Mock push.sh must NOT write to stderr (per test_isolation_contract), so only execute-push.py warn-path risk output appears in captured bytes. Confirmed by iter3 Codex live probe. Unchanged from iter3.

passed_checks.title: AC2 vs AC3 non-overlap: separate coverage of non-head_sha vs head_sha mismatch
passed_checks.details: AC2 GIVEN explicitly states 'head_sha is set to current HEAD so step 9 would pass — only step 7 fires'. AC3 GIVEN explicitly states 'all fields EXCEPT head_sha are valid' and 'head_sha is deliberately different from current HEAD so step 9 fires'. No conditions overlap. Each AC tests a distinct failure path.

passed_checks.title: commands/push.md scope: Step 4 MUST NOT language already present
passed_checks.details: Live read of commands/push.md line 146: 'The orchestrator MUST NOT read, validate, or unlink the push-analyst grant manually,' — MUST NOT language is present. AC9 positive assertion ('grep -i MUST NOT commands/push.md returns at least one match') passes on current file. Step 4 update is confirmed in place.

passed_checks.title: AC9 positive assertion scoped correctly
passed_checks.details: AC9 THEN: 'grep -i MUST NOT commands/push.md returns at least one match near Step 4 heading'. The current file has this match at line 146 within the Step 4 section. The positive assertion is satisfied by correct Step 4 content.

passed_checks.title: execute-push.py referenced in push.md (AC9 third check)
passed_checks.details: AC9 check: 'grep_execute_push_py_present: true'. Live verification: commands/push.md line 183 currently contains 'python3 /root/.claude/scripts/execute-push.py' — grep 'execute-push.py' returns a match. After M13 dev update this will still be present (with tilde form). AC9 third check passes on current file and will pass after dev update.

ba_validation_dimensions.evidence_quality: pass — all factual claims backed by source reads with line citations (commands/push.md line 146 for MUST NOT language, line 183 for /root/ form, push.sh lines 137-150 for sentinel schema, agents/push-analyst.md for grant schema). Live greps performed and exit codes recorded.
ba_validation_dimensions.scope_alignment: pass — scope correctly covers scripts/execute-push.py (create) and commands/push.md Steps 4+5 (modify). MoSCoW items M1-M13, S1, C1 are internally consistent with the described scope. The single HEAD drift check at step 9 is correctly specified; no --pre-head arg is introduced.
ba_validation_dimensions.investigation_completeness: pass — BA traced sentinel schema from push.sh:137-150, traced grant schema from agents/push-analyst.md grant fields, identified the timing-window root cause (&&-chaining allows interstitial modifications between sentinel write and push.sh read), and correctly identified os.execv as the atomicity mechanism.
ba_validation_dimensions.affected_file_accuracy: pass — scripts/execute-push.py (to create) and commands/push.md (Steps 4+5) are the correct affected files. hooks/push.sh is correctly identified as read-only infra. File paths exist and contain the code BA claims.
ba_validation_dimensions.spec_text_vs_execution_drift: FAIL — one AC verification recipe cannot execute the literal check it claims: AC10 THEN asserts tilde form 'NOT /root/.claude/' but the check object provides no recipe to verify that grep -F '/root/.claude/scripts/execute-push.py' commands/push.md exits 1. A dev who adds the tilde form alongside the existing /root/ form at line 183 would satisfy all three current AC10 checks while violating M13. The 'NOT /root/.claude/' assertion in the THEN text is mechanically unverifiable by the supplied check recipes.

codex_consult.feedback_summary: Codex raised 7 findings. Finding 3 (AC10 /root absence gap) is the blocking issue in this report — Codex confirmed that AC10's positive grep for tilde form does not exclude the /root/ form, and old_and_pattern_absent checks an orthogonal set of patterns. Findings 1, 2, 4, 5, 6, 7 were adjudicated as out_of_scope or OBSERVATION_ONLY: Finding 1 (AC9 positive not Step-4-specific — existing MUST NOT language at line 146 satisfies this regardless of dev's Step 4 update); Finding 2 (AC9 negative pattern under-inclusive for unlink "$GRANT_PATH" and os.remove() — coverage hardening beyond iter3 scope); Finding 4 (AC2/3/4 GIVEN omits CLAUDE_SESSION_ID — documentation gap, implicit in session_id field matching); Finding 5 (branch drift when current_branch != --branch — explicit out-of-scope per spec constraint 'Single HEAD drift check at step 9 ONLY'); Finding 6 (--auto forwarding not mechanically verified — already logged as iter3 out_of_scope_observation); Finding 7 (existing implementation does not have --branch — OBSERVATION_ONLY per scope: this is BA spec validation not implementation validation).
codex_consult.feedback_incorporated: Codex Finding 3 promoted to blocking finding ISSUE-NEW-1 in this report. All other findings adjudicated and routed to out_of_scope_observations.

out_of_scope_observations.observation: Codex Finding 1: AC9 positive assertion 'grep -i MUST NOT commands/push.md returns at least one match near Step 4 heading' is satisfied by existing line 146 even without the M12 Step 4 update that adds 'sole'. The positive assertion does not verify the new 'sole' semantic specifically. BA may optionally tighten to 'grep -i sole.*handler\|sole.*responsible' near Step 4 heading, or accept the existing MUST NOT language as sufficient positive evidence.

Codex Finding 2: AC9 negative pattern 'os\.unlink\(|rm -f .*grant' is under-inclusive — a developer could write shell 'unlink "$GRANT_PATH"' or Python 'os.remove(grant_path)' in a code block and neither pattern would match. Coverage hardening beyond iter3 scope; the spec's concern is preventing manual unlink instructions from re-appearing in code blocks.

Codex Finding 4: AC2, AC3, AC4 GIVEN clauses do not explicitly state CLAUDE_SESSION_ID must be set, but the grant's session_id field matching implicitly requires it. This is a documentation completeness gap. BA could add 'CLAUDE_SESSION_ID set' to the GIVEN of AC2/AC3/AC4 for consistency with AC1/AC5.

Codex Finding 5: Spec validates grant.branch == --branch but does not require verifying current_branch == --branch. If user switches branch to another pointing at same HEAD between grant creation and execv, sentinel is written with wrong branch. Spec explicitly constrains to single HEAD drift check at step 9; current-branch validation is out of this scope.

Codex Finding 6: --auto forwarding (M10 Must Have) has no AC that specifically captures $2/$@ in the mock invocation. Mock capture protocol writes only $1 (remote) to MOCK_CAPTURE_PATH. A separate AC or extension to AC1 should verify --auto is present in execv argv when the flag is passed. Previously logged in iter3 out_of_scope_observations.

Codex Finding 7: The existing scripts/execute-push.py at /dev/shm/dev-workspace/dot-claude/scripts/execute-push.py does not have a --branch argument in argparse (only --request-id, --repo-hash, --remote, --auto). This would cause AC1 to fail at implementation time. OBSERVATION_ONLY for BA spec validation — BA spec validation does not verify the implementation, only the spec's internal consistency and verifiability.

qa.approval_notes: REJECTED — iteration 4 resolves both iter3 blocking issues (AC9 false-positive grep and AC10 multiline-args grep are confirmed fixed). However, Codex adversarial review surfaces one new blocking spec_text_vs_execution_drift gap: AC10 THEN text asserts tilde form 'NOT /root/.claude/' but the check object supplies no recipe to verify the /root/ form is absent. The fix is minimal: add one negative check entry to the AC10 check object ('old_root_form_absent_exit_code: 1, verification_command: grep -F python3 /root/.claude/scripts/execute-push.py commands/push.md'). All other dimensions pass. Re-submit as iteration 5 with only this single check addition applied.
